status_legend.active: The one feature currently being worked.
status_legend.blocked: Progress is stopped by a documented blocker.
status_legend.passing: Verification passed and evidence is recorded.

features.title: Provider egress allowlisting
features.user_visible_behavior: Internet-enabled agent runs can be restricted to reviewed provider and package endpoints instead of unrestricted egress.
verification: Threat model updated
Provider network mode is integrated into normal runs
Smoke harness proves host allowlist proxy on an internal Apple container network
Provider allowlist behavior tested
Runtime smoke proves denied and allowed network paths
Docs state exact limitations
Blocked provider hosts are surfaced to the user
Allowed hosts resolving to unsafe local addresses are denied
Provider policy decisions are persisted for later review
Host allowlist decisions can be explained before rerun
Provider endpoint matrix backs bundled hosts and known explicit-review hosts
Blocked-host review groups run id, rule, count, reason, and suggested next action
Live smoke harness can test bundled provider hosts for a selected profile
Empty allowlist behavior is regression-tested for internet, internal, provider, and proxy policy paths

Bundled provider hosts now come from the reviewed provider endpoint matrix. Broad path-sensitive, telemetry, update, reporting, and weakly sourced hosts remain explicit `--provider-host` review items. Provider policy decisions are logged for review, and blocked provider targets are grouped after runs with run id, counts, reasons, matched rules, and suggested next actions.

features.title: Host-side auth broker boundary
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven auth status` and `runhaven auth explain AGENT` describe the credential broker boundary without reading or printing secrets. Codex can opt into a host-side API-key broker with `--network provider --codex-api-key-broker-env NAME`.
verification: Static auth broker metadata exists for every bundled profile
Codex API-key broker reads a named host environment variable only during real runs
Codex broker injects temporary custom-provider config and a placeholder token into the guest without exposing the raw key
Codex broker only accepts provider-network clients and forwards Responses API create requests
Codex broker records secret-free decisions in `runhaven auth log`
Optional Codex broker smoke harness skips without a key and runs only with an explicit disposable key environment variable
`runhaven auth status` prints broker status without reading credential stores or environment values
`runhaven auth explain AGENT` prints profile-specific auth guidance without brokered secrets
JSON auth output is deterministic and secret-free
Docs state the Codex prototype status, remaining design-only provider status, trust boundary, non-goals, and future acceptance criteria
Source ledger records current provider auth references
evidence: 2026-06-15: Added `src/runhaven/auth_broker.py` with static per-profile broker metadata.
2026-06-15: Added `runhaven auth status` and `runhaven auth explain AGENT` with optional JSON output.
2026-06-15: Focused auth CLI tests passed and verified fake `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` values were not printed.
2026-06-15: Manual `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m runhaven auth status` and `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m runhaven auth explain codex` checks passed.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 79 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build.
2026-06-15: Added opt-in Codex API-key broker prototype, focused planner/proxy/CLI tests, and official Codex configuration source notes.
2026-06-15: Added secret-free Codex broker decision logging, `runhaven auth log`, and optional `scripts/codex_broker_smoke.py` live-smoke harness.
features.notes: Codex has an opt-in host-side API-key broker prototype. Other agent auth brokers remain design-only. Auth status/explain/log do not inspect credential stores or environment values. `plan` and `run --dry-run` do not read the named Codex API-key environment variable. Broker logs omit token values, request bodies, and environment variable names.

features.title: Secret-free run observability
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven runs list`, `runhaven runs show RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs log RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs diff RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs active`, `runhaven runs status RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs attach RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs logs-follow RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs stop RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs kill RUN_ID`, `runhaven runs repair RUN_ID`, and `runhaven runs repair --all` show recent actual agent runs and control active runs without exposing commands or secrets. Repair commands also expose scriptable JSON summaries.
verification: Actual standard runs append a secret-free record to `runs.jsonl`
Provider runs append a run record that shares the run id with provider egress and auth broker logs
Run records summarize provider policy, auth broker, cleanup outcomes, and git workspace changes
`runhaven runs list` shows recent run summaries
`runhaven runs show RUN_ID` prints text and JSON for one run
`runhaven runs log RUN_ID` joins the run record with matching provider policy and auth broker entries
`runhaven runs diff RUN_ID` prints a live git diff only after recorded metadata still matches the live workspace
`runhaven runs active` lists currently active RunHaven markers in text and JSON without requiring Apple `container` access
`runhaven runs status RUN_ID` shows sanitized active marker and live Apple `container inspect` state without raw process arguments, environment, or mounts
`runhaven runs attach RUN_ID` opens a guarded Apple `container exec` shell or command only for an active RunHaven-owned named container
`runhaven runs logs-follow RUN_ID` follows recent and live Apple `container logs` output only for an active RunHaven-owned named container
`runhaven runs stop RUN_ID` stops only an active RunHaven-owned named Apple container
`runhaven runs kill RUN_ID` hard-stops only an active RunHaven-owned named Apple container and records killed runs as `killed`
`runhaven runs repair RUN_ID` removes only stale active markers whose recorded RunHaven-owned Apple container is confirmed missing
`runhaven runs repair --all` applies the same confirmed-missing guard across valid active markers and returns nonzero when any marker cannot be verified
`runhaven runs repair RUN_ID --json` and `runhaven runs repair --all --json` print secret-free repair results, counts, and exit codes for automation
Run records and active-run markers omit diffs, file contents, prompts, command lines, agent arguments, environment variable names, environment values, request bodies, and token values

features.notes: `runs list/show/log/diff/active/status/attach/logs-follow/stop/kill/repair` are the first run dashboard and recovery slices. `runs diff` prints live git output on demand and does not store patches. `runs active` recovers current run ids from secret-free markers. `runs status` uses curated Apple `container inspect` output. `runs attach` uses Apple `container exec` because the pinned local CLI has no installed attach plugin. `runs logs-follow` uses Apple `container logs --follow`. `runs stop`, `runs kill`, and `runs repair` work only for active runs with a current active marker. `runs repair --all` handles multiple valid active markers with the same confirmed-missing guard. Repair JSON output includes only result status, counts, run id, container name, inspect return code, marker removal state, and exit code.

features.title: Guided first-run setup
features.user_visible_behavior: `runhaven setup` runs prerequisite checks, prints exact fixes when the host is not ready, shows the next image build, plan, and run commands for a selected agent, and explains goal-based network choices without mutating the machine.
verification: `runhaven setup` runs the same prerequisite checks as `doctor`
`runhaven setup` returns nonzero and prints exact remedies when prerequisites fail
`runhaven setup --agent AGENT` prints profile-specific image build, plan, and run commands when prerequisites pass
`runhaven setup` prints local-only, provider-only, package install, and unrestricted internet network guidance
`runhaven setup` does not install, start services, build images, run agents, or mount workspaces
evidence: 2026-06-15: Focused setup CLI tests first failed because `setup` was not a valid subcommand, then passed after adding the non-mutating guided setup flow.

2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 154 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding `runhaven setup`. Manual `runhaven setup --agent shell` smoke, JSON validation, Markdown link check, platform scan, and `git diff --check` also passed.

2026-06-15: Focused setup network guidance test first failed because `setup` did not print a network-choice section, then passed after adding local-only, provider-only, package install, and unrestricted internet guidance.
2026-06-15: `PYTHON=<temporary-venv-python> ./init.sh` passed with compileall, 155 unit tests, pin check, ruff, mypy, and build after adding setup network guidance.
features.notes: `setup` intentionally does not call Apple `container system start`, build images, run agents, or write state. Future setup work can add workspace-scope guidance and credential-path explanations.
